steps.title: Spot the parallel structure
steps.content: The sentence includes “and monitor seasonal ice zones,” which signals multiple parallel actions. The blank adds another action (“travel to greater depths”), making three items to coordinate: measure variables, travel to depths, and monitor zones.

steps.title: Apply the series punctuation rule
steps.content:
Option B provides the correct series punctuation: commas between items and “and” before the last.

This creates “can measure a wider range of variables, travel to greater depths, and monitor seasonal ice zones.” The other options violate this rule with a colon (A), missing punctuation (C), or a semicolon before “and” (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
